Notes: Club-class #6, AAT.
1st AAT. Not too shabby but came in a minute under time - dumb
Late to the party.
Juniors #2. Narrowly avoided a relight scraping at 750ft with 100l of water. Then whiplash, first half lame and second half stonk.

Absolute mayhem. Scraping, stuck being blown towards Cranfield's ATZ in gusty thermals, vis so bad you could barely see the next cloud, and then my stick fell off... 

Got in the groove for a nice 2nd leg but then it degenerated and I ran out of skill.
Got to EAW late and it was crap, otherwise a superb day.
Turn points a bit sketchy but otherwise reasonable. Overcast by the end
Strong stuff! Barely any worrisome moments, good fun.
Club class #1. Took a wrong street so the 2nd half was playing catch-up.
First 500! Followed immediately by a road retrieve to Buckingham.
Shoot the task-setter!

Actually had a tricky time on the first leg, a lot of messy cloud which didn't really show me where the lift was. Got a lot better after Edgehill, streets galore! Best visibility I've ever seen, could see the London skyscrapers from Cambridge!
A little overset
Another superb day generally, unfortunately survival modes at Melton (overcast) and Oxford (blue) each took a ragged chunk of speed. Biggest triangle yet!
Thought I could do better than 1.3kts and was proven wrong in short order.
Squeaky & scrappy.
Dunstable ICL #1.
First and last 50k were a bit iffy.
Club-class #4. Spent too long playing start games, tut tut. Smidge too late for the last thermal home.
I drew the shape of GB with my trace. And the stick didn't fall off.
Juniors #1. Lame start but strong climbs.
Loads of good climbs but only after I finished the damn task 🤷‍♂️
pain
Enormous deviations to begin as there were only about 3 streets in all east Anglia. Squeezy final glide.
Really good apart from a soft patch between about Mansfield and Wittering.  Glad I flew.  Thanks to M8 and 841 for the task; pity I couldn't catch you up!
Quite possibly the slowest XC flight I've done since I owned a Skylark 3.  Thanks (!) to Robert Theil for the task.

Tremendous in East Anglia, despite the low cloudbase.
Watched 899 get over-confident as he passed GRL, then got over-confident myself as I passed him...  Took me too long to dig myself out of that, and a further delay in a soft spot around Didcot.

Quite good apart from soft patches at Newport Pag and Bury.
Some instrumentation challenges meant I was flying too slowly.  Some laziness meant I was flying too light.
Thanks to 205, 899, 700, MB and M8 for the task I didn't do.

Very good until just short of Bidford, where I got out of phase with the weather.
Became marginal around Silverstone on the way back, with long glides to questionable clouds.
Director's cut. FAI turnpoints around the distance-handicapped task for the last day of the ever-popular Cambridge Cloud Rally

Started too late, went the wrong way, needed to be back early.
Ballistic until about Edgehill, then increasing murk, overcast and spreadout.  Very glad I hadn't declared Malvern as originally intended; at Moreton I was surrounded by a band of rain.
Tricky back to about Buckingham on the way back, then ballistic all the way home.
Great fun while it was great fun.
Good in parts.  Thanks to Wendy and Mike in 72 for the task.

Sweaty.
Could have gone further west, but to go beyond Elmswell would have been foolhardy (lots of spreadout, indecipherable convergences and blue holes).
Lift in unexpected places, and lots of sink where I didn't want it.
Wouldn't have flown at all, but fed up with watching everyone else have all the fun.
Frustrating and tiring.

I never quite trusted the day, but actually it continued to deliver 4-5 knot average climbs until the last 30km or so.  The areas of spreadout and grot gradually got larger, and the sunny gaps got smaller.
High cover turned out not to be a big problem, later it cleared and base went to 5.3K
Good all around the task, base late on in East Anglia 6k, might have stretched too a 500 but late starting at GRL
Low base to start, gradually improved to 4K, lots of good climbs even under spreadout on the way back, it was either keep on track or divert to the sun at Northampton, on track it was.
The wind destroyed most thermals, seem to end up with around 3kts average, base in East Anglia did reach 5K.
Highlight was the streeting from HUS most of the way to TIB, then good climbs even under hi cover( cold air advection?)
Did not have the faith that the leg to OXF could be done, hence the abandonment
ICL
ICL, post cold front, base over 5k
Bit of a grovel on track to NWK, after, detoured to the east to use the sea breeze front, base then 5.5K, getting late so went home.
Probably started to early, very narrow thermals for most of the flight
Very windy, broken thermals, but good streeting if you could stay high
Strange areas that were very difficult to cross, in the good bits base 5K with 5Kt climbs
Had cu all the way up, but blued out at Retford on the way back, luckily base was over 4.5K so you could keep over the hot spots, even so had a couple of low scrapes
Not quite as good as it looked
Thought it would easier, but difficult to centre, broken thermals, and a sign saying don't get below 2000feet, exhausted (come back Mark), good company and good flying from Richard!!
Difficult day i thought, stiff bum for Dave, good times and bad as usual but happy to have got round !! Thanks Dave for your help 👍
Quite difficult in places, second leg very good, last leg looked more difficult than it was, surprisingly good flight considering the morning weather and late start !
Good for the first 75% of the flight, managed to get low at Newmarket and struggled to get back up ,,  eventually crawled away and managed to get back,, 😀 thanks Stuart for your help !
Just a bit of fun with George  ,great lift, fabulous cloud streets, glad we turned for home when we did !!! Made a good decision for a change!!
Good flight !! Thanks for the task everyone,, great to have Matijhe to help with the hard work !

Slow to get going (me and the thermals), then got really good from thr first turnpoint. Then typically, spreadout from Newmarket, slowed us up but altogether a great flight with George flying really well, beautiful views over the coastline, thanks Paul for the different task!
Good and difficult day, mostly good though fortunately!! First cross country for Antony, flew extremely well and great eyesight!!
First leg okay, then top cover made life difficult until Northampton,  rest was easy , bit slow but was trying to look after Lee's stomach!! Thanks to Mike and Wendy for the perfect task !

Was going really well, (where have i heard that before?), untill Olney at 1300 feet , hand on the solo knob, found 0.9 of a thermal, magnificently ground away untill 3000 and then legged it for home ,yippee!! Thanks George for excellent flying today, thanks to the tasksetters forthe task ...
Fantastic flight with Steve Phillips who let me do all the flying , bless him 🙏 Fabulous visibility  very strong but turbulent thermals,, happy 😊 days !!
Fantastic flight ,, never even got low !! ,, Good as always, to fly with Jonathan!!!

Great to fly with Markie,,,,made things a lot easier...
A great flight until we got diverted 3 miles to the west of Wittering, hey ho, any excuse!!

Brilliant.. fantastic 7 knot thermals, beautiful visibility,,, then,,, dead as a doornail from Newmarket,,, loads of avgas,, got home eventually,, 18 knot wind at times,, thanks Jonathon for teamwork !!
Pre-solo first 50 km cross-country!
We found it really easy. A truly great flight with no issues, brilliant visability and lots of 6 knot thermals.

Good flight really in a lot of places, good helpful flying from Antony, getting older by the flight though 
 😀

Mark and Andy flew together as usual. The day got better and better, so we were able to romp round the 308km triangle at a rather modest 76 kph. We found this rather slow progress inexplicable. We blamed each other, but it was actually because we didn't want Robert Theil to get depressed if we beat him.

Brilliant flight with Andy.  Tricky little start, but once out west the streets were forming up and we even got a 9 kt thermal (admittedly for a few seconds only!).  The way back home was very straightforward, blueing out a little for last few kms.  339 kms at 95 kph.  A memorable flight.
Just a lovely Old gentleman's " flight. Beautiful views
column87: hopefully not the last flight of the season!!"

Notes: Another great flight, good strong easy thermals,5 knot averages , cloud base almost 5000 feet ,great routing by Mark, happy days 😀
ICL competition
ICL COMP
Welcome back Mark Dalton,,, fabulous flight ,lots of good climbs and fantastic streets, great to sit in the back again !!

Well, major disaster at the start by falling out after the winch launch ,.Irritated, went to the aerowtow queue, waited,, my turn came and tuggie then left me to re-fuel.  Irritated, went back to the winch queue,,, got away and went as fast as possible,, good day in the end !
Kept falling into holes and having to scrabble out.
Started out declaring BIC Saltby, but murk above 3000 beyond St Neots was 'orrible. So I came back and did a shorter task.
Very hot and sticky. Thermals no easier than they've been on other days recently (well, for me anyway!)
First flight since I got my medical back - just an exploratory flight.
As predicted, very grey on approach to GRM. Tiptoed in and out, picking up whatever lift I could find under grey clouds. But did it!
Still slow, but completed. Idea for Bicester as first tp thanks to the group who planned a 400. I expected to be slow so didn't join them on that.
I think I've decided to go for the slowest time for any task, whatever the distance!
Another epic in my fly tasks as slow as possible". At least I got back before the rain

As predicted, very grey on approach to GRM. Tiptoed in and out, picking up whatever lift I could find under grey clouds. But did it! Now showing Shark rather than Ventus (last year's loan from Tim Dews - thanks Tim)

As part of G's coaching course, did a follow" with Chris Gill leading the way. My most enjoyable XC flight so far by far... learned so much. So that's how you do it!!! (these ladder points belong to Chris really). Had a slight faff at the end to avoid the height penalty (thought I'd messed it up but turns out not)

Club Name: wave and thermal in four days of flying. Thanks to G
Surname: Chris and of course Rod for an amazing set-up."

Notes: A beautiful trundle around North Wales. Faffed about trying to get into the wave at the start. Then found it difficult to match the cloud scape with the lift. Either way, a fantastic day out. Missed Corwen out as not confident to stay above the cloud in a cold soaked glider...
For various reasons found that hard work... (put another way, the glider-to-atmosphere interface unit" wasn't working very well i.e. me!)"

Launched from Audley End. The forecasts suggested that 11am to 2pm in East Anglia would be the optimum window. It was spot on. Thoroughly enjoyed leg 1... had a faff on leg 2 as it started to spread out and I made a meal of leaping between the energy lines. Leg 3 was the most fun ever. Found a superb line and nearly managed leg 3 with no turning (took a cheeky top up for some margin north of Haverhill).
Launched from Audley End

Launched from Audley End. 
Flew to run the engine but turned into a nice afternoon out...

Launched from Audley End.
Was all going swimmingly (well, leg 1 slow, enjoyed leg 2 and the first half of leg 3) until I failed to spot the lack of sun on the ground towards the end of leg 3. Should have been more cautious and stayed high... anyway, fun while it lasted...
Launched from Audley End.

Launched from Audley End (had planned to fly from GRL earlier but then life got in the way)
A massive blob of overcast parked itself over my start point which was a blessing in disguise as I had to get on with it (instead of my usual indecision) 
Leg 1 superb conditions... leg 2 I had a faff before the last turn point... last leg looked a bit flat but was actually ok. Chuffed with my fastest ever XC speed until I saw you lot post your epic speeds. Onwards and upwards... :)

Not sure why the task doesn't match the declaration. Might be because there was a power outage (when I knocked the switch before the start)... either way, more difficult than I imagined.... but still good fun. Thanks to SJ (Chris) for marking a thermal near BSE that saved my day...
Rain stopped play
Post-flight had to clean flies off the trailing edge... again...
Launched from Audley End.
Club 100. My find a street to snort along on the downwind leg" plan didn't come to fruition. Hence  painfully slow but good to fly before the sky filled in."

Launched from Audley End
Thermal of the day thanks to Snetterton Racetrack... 1500' in 4 minutes. Shame they weren't all like that...
Launched from Audley End.

Delayed start due tech issue. Got airborne about 2 hours too late but undeterred headed into a good looking sky. An RAF C17 put me off my stride after Lakenheath asked me to turn right (thus avoiding the best looking cloud of the day)... their ILS practice clearly didn't consider my task. After that, someone (I blame Skysight) turned off the sky... after that a mess... I thought at least Bury St Edmunds would throw me back into the sky but alas no... I had to start Greta to get me home... turns out that SS forecast the deterioration perfectly. I just misread the forecast...

Launched from Audley End.
On the wrong side of the country today but planned to fly in the bit that SS said would work away from the North Sea air. Slow but enjoyable.
Cambridge Gliding Club Cloud Rally. Task had additional first turnpoint at Cambridge South.  Actual task was 220k net of variable barrels
Logger upload stated I did not complete 1st TP whereas SeeYou trace shows I entered the barrel.
Almost cocked up the final glide.
Never too late for a shakedown flight.
Doh.

Difficult start but managed to get away after much teeth grinding and swearing. Although we didn't complete the task it was nevertheless a good day. Due to old age Andy surrendered at Peterborough and we turned for home. A good decision.  Thanks Andy for another great day.
Another 300km attempt abandoned after the sky in front greyed out. It was probably possible to finish but I didn't really want to risk ending up in a field at this time of year.

Great day out with 4X.  We had a good run down to Edgehill but lots of big deviations and slow spots on the way back. The climbs got a lot weaker and it blued out in front of us so we abandoned before Kings Lynn and came home.
Task 2. Picked a better task to fit the streets which worked really well until I got low. Nothing much working below 2000 so was a bit of a challenge to scrape home.
ICL Day 1. Would've been better with a bit of ballast but no one knew how strong it was going to be. A few slow spots but generally quite good.
Slow and safe start to my XC season. Finished too low to climb away so had to take a second launch to do another task.

Started too early. Everything was working much better on the way back from Frome. Also shouldn't have been so lazy and actually bothered to put some water in. 
Still another fun day out in 296 despite all that.
Interesting first day on the Downs. Had a nice close look at Parham before the thermals started working again. Glad to make it home.
Great flight.  Couple of 8 knotters. turned a bit blue after the last turn point, but thermals still working.  Very satisfying day.
Declared about 400  km, but gave up at around the 250 km mark.  Flew around for a bit before returning home.

Tricky little day.  Hard work, mainly because we couldn't get above 3,400 feet, and usually below 2,500 ft agl !  Got low twice, which slowed things a bit, but satisfying to get home.  Cambridge radar enjoyed relaying the fact that there were '17 gliders in the air' in the vicinity of their airfield.

First XC flight of year (long story). Didn't like forecast for East so stayed West, and strategically underset given recency. Conditions very kind - good-looking clouds worked, some streeting. All good till got back East towards GRL and it turned iffy and had a overlong grub around for get home height. Slow, but very happy.

Thanks to David Tew for this oddly shaped task, allegedly to be in the good weather...
If pulling off tow in lift only to have it immediately disappear didn't set the tone for the day then getting away from 500 feet before the start and losing half my body weight in sweat in the conditions certainly did...
Started well, and mercifully quite high, but Skeffington,. to quote David was 'orrible and blue.

Well, it was a game of two halves! First leg was  about 130kph, telling myself not to climb in paltry 4 knot thermals when there were so much better climbs to be had. It got slower and slower on the second leg and towards Eastwell, the top cover just closed up. Initially some Cu embedded in that top cover, but eventually it just died altogether. Crept round Eastwell, and glid towards climbs further South, but ground came up to meet me beyond Melton Mowbray.
Not so easy initially with bumpy, tumbly thermals but but mid-way found a couple of good strong climbs to 5000'.

Good climbs available much of the time except for a patch 3/4 of the way back from KLY to UPW. The clouds looked the same but didn't deliver the same lift. Finally a good climb near UPW got us back on final glide.
No comment.
Not a big task but the first x-c completed in Z18 since 2022 so a degree of satisfaction.
Last day of the GCG 2024 Cloud Rally comp. Funster day 9 completed.

CGC cloud rally - Funster class, day 7 task B. 
A blue day which was a bit daunting at first. Managed to get to first TP, but it took a long time and so decided to go back in case conditions worsened as predicted. Turned out to be strong thermals beyond the expected window. A fun day out, nevertheless :)
CGC Cloud rally Funsters Day 2. didn't get very far on task, but far enough to get out of my comfort zone!
Another go at going to Spalding...
Set the task, but couldn't get to grips with the blue day - so didn't get very far!
CGC club task
First time to Crowland and in sight of the Wash :)

I did not expect such a long task today so I didn't end up finishing it. First two legs went pretty well staying high despite blue holes, then got low over Newport which lost me ages. Into wind leg was slow, but actually quite a nice cloud street. 110km to Waterbeach was mostly OK, but got really low (1200ft) south of Northampton after having to avoid the huge thermal over Northampton due to airspace. I managed to climb away again though. After turning I decided to land back at Gransden as the day was getting late and I didn't fancy 80km into wind.

Quite a windy day to say the least, 22kts at flying height made the into wind legs a bit of a nightmare. Also my first AAT so not tactically my best work but I got round which I was really happy with. Apart from a serious tactical error at the second turn point a successful flight. And the start sector was some of the funnest flying I've done, absolute chaos with all the gliders under the same cloud street but wow that cloud street was insane.

What a stunning day. The plan was HusBos-Bicester. I got cold feet (literally and metaphorically) halfway to Bicester so turned back but returned to find two incredible cloud streets that I just shot along a few times. Had to pull the airbrakes after 6 hours

My first involuntary example of travel by glider. Starting the task at 15:02 might have been a little late and meant that I had a nice encounter with sea breeze which was rather detrimental. Amazing field for a first landout though and really nice farmer.

I decided this morning that I wouldn't go cross country today, but it seems the English weather had other plans. The lack of clouds at the end of the day meant I didn't go as far but another amazing flight

I was really relieved to get round this task, landing back 40 minutes after the thermals were predicted to stop. Not particularly fast but my longest declared flight by far. First final glide ever was OK although I ended up far too high at the airfield.

Definitely not as strong as yesterday, the strong wind was still a major problem and meant that I found thermals almost impossible to centre. Start sector was a bit trash so decided to start quite low and then found a smashing thermal on the first leg. First leg was good, second was also good with quite a nice cloud street reaching almost the second turn point. Having turned that though there were no suitable cloud streets so I spent a while trying to gain height and in the end just being pushed back towards the turn point so just started on the leg but some atrocious sink put me straight into Lyveden.
I had declared Bury St. Edmunds as the eastern turnpoint but changed my mind and extended to Framlingham.

Really nice up to about Bicester, then towards Oxford bigger gaps, more clouds didn't deliver. This got worse, much worse on the second leg towards Osbournby. I made an unusually big detour towards the East to stay with reasonable-looking clouds, as spreadout crept in from the West, but in the end, Osbournby just seemed out of reach. Getting to Mendlesham was all right, although again big gaps in pretty dead air had to be crossed, but the way home to Gransden looked and was really tricky. A scrawny-looking cloud NE of Newmarket delivered the height needed for the final glide, which was basically through dead air.
Quite windy with 30-40 kph, but mostly good, until I got low on the last leg into wind and lost a lot of time drifting backwards. A lesson to remember.

Got going a bit late, unfortunately. Great conditions on the way to the first turnpoint and up to north of Thetford, then a bit softer (I thought), then very nice again around the second turnpoint Retford. Increasingly blue and a bit trickier on the way to Northampton South. Some encounters with competition gliders helped find thermals, as many cloudlets didn't deliver.

Slow start on the first leg to Calvert Rail Junction, which I then abandoned to try the conditions further north, going towards Eastwell. This, too, didn't go too well - quite good initially, but then it looked blue and unreliable. Going towards East Anglia, conditions were good, but nice-looking clouds sometimes wouldn't deliver. I found it a tricky day, with inhomogeneous conditions and lots of weak climbs with just a few nice ones.

A tricky flight, perhaps the most difficult I had this year. Brisk wind at about 35 kph, some good climbs at first to lure me in, but increasingly hard to find and to centre beyond Bedford. Bicester seemed like a world away. After rounding Spalding, the situation looked pretty hopeless with lots of spreadout, little sun on the ground, and the occasional sick and unpromising Cu. Somehow, I got back, but I don't think the full task was on.

Up towards the second turnpoint Stourport and also the start of the third leg were a bit soft, then good again apart from the area around Ely, last turnpoint, where there was pretty much nothing to get me onto glide. Diverting towards Cambridge Marshall's, a weak thermal near Oakington, in the blue, gave the necessary height to get home.
Some trouble with high cover up to Corby, then good, then very tricky around Tuxford, fairly nice for the rest of the trip (but not enough time to finish the task).
Second XC since returning to gliding. Need to get faster!
First cross-country for amost 25 years! Nice to have got back into it.

Hus bos leg of Icl day 2.
Novice task

Cloud rally fund yet task day 2.

Very tricky round lavenham and got low but otherwise a fun day out

Cloud Rally Friday Funster Class
First time flying in the blue. 

Great fun!
Cambridge Cloud Rally

Slow, but generally found things where I needed.
Cambridge very helpful with transponder, letting me climb in their ILS and routing traffic around me.  But not sure they get the competitive spirit, as they told the next glider through where I had been climbing !!!
It was desperation during a bad run of weather, but satisfying and I didn't have to visit Great Malvern again!

New CGC strategy, send pilots round in both directions, then we can decide after which was the better choice!!

About 3 really good climbs, the rest were all fickle and hard to core.
Lost out a lot on the final leg.
But still, a nice day out.
Lovely trip with the gang
Out of sync with the weather - dug myself out of 2 holes, then get nicer after Bury - good task setting from 28

worth every penny of the winch launch
good fun racing the others around - I need to fly 15m next time
hats off to 4X who was brave enough to declare and complete 400k
Lovely scenic trip to the Peak District - cu all the way, just blu’ed out south of Peterborough

thank you to 205 for the task, which is further then I had intended

left GRL under low base of 2500, which went up as we went further West and generally decent climbs

but diversion to Northampton on the way back as things spread out and a low level ground inspection - but thankfully, switching the engine electrics on delivered a thermal, and got back ok in the end

Hard work, with soggy non-round thermals.

Very unimaginative on the task, but suited the weather

Thank you to Mike for sharing the fun!

I found it hard work - broken climbs and strong wind - better after Northampton into East Anglia and back to Marham, but then things were dying and many clouds which looked OK didn't deliver, so limped back, then got a stonking climb just as I got on to glide.

slow!
left GRL with low base and soft climbs - got better after the M1 - but a bit soft again 30K into and out of GRM -  then romp again for the middle 50K and a useful climb at MK which got me onto glide into a dead looking sky

looking forward to flying on a nice day, sometime!
thermals a bit scrappy initially, then fabulous after Newmarket - should have stayed in the East, as abandoned Oxford after Olney due to approaching front - big respect to V11 for his great flight!

Fabulous conditions, after it got started. 6000’ base in East Anglia and strong climbs.
Lovely day out with 28.

Hard work - awful vis - lost out after Manton, hit a thermal after short engine burst - thought we would be fine soaring back after that, but it was hard work.  Thermals small, gusty and broken.  Never trust a soarable southerly!

Had wondered whether it was worth flying today ....   Still, good company if not a lot of fun!

murky and a bit spread out in the middle of the country - found it hard to get reliable cores, and many clouds didn't work, apart from nice 7knot at MK at the end of street - got better on leg south near Salty and finally towards the end started to feel good cores - should have started later

Had to wait at GRL until the band of top cover had gone through, then a pleasant romp, just a bit slow around Eastwell due to spread out. Felt like another Cloud Rally flight, flying with the usual gang.
actually a nice flight- extended to club task from declared GRM - but but sadly didn't manage to limp to the sunshine on the wayback as we went into a spread out sky
Not my day, well done to those who romped around!

Perfect task to fit into the day - thank you to Phil Atkin!
Great company from Mark, who did the largest part of the flying as well.
Wouldn't have flown, but looks the best window for this week - fingers crossed we get better weather soon.

2 out of 3 ain't bad!
on the first leg, soft climbs which were hard to core - second leg had much stronger climbs, but a lot of spread out - continued towards Newark as it looked better then going home, when the balance changed and Newark looked bleak I turned back - on glide, having eaten all my final glide grapes, I saw it was looking better to the East with more sun on the ground, so ended up going to Bury in the end, which was quite straightforward

I couldn't understand the forecasts this morning, but in reality, it did pretty much do what what forecast

Conditions not great at GRL, and after nearly landing back a couple of times, I decided the only thing to do was to start the task!
In reality first leg was OK, second was hard until after Market Harborough and then the rest OK, but had to do an annoying divert near Bevior to connect on the last leg. 
But it felt like desperation and didn't really feel like fun until half way round, however satisfying!
Top Birthday!

Fell down from the winch, so spent the rest of the day trying to catchup.
Sometimes it Isn't all about the speed. 
Strong climbs, but hard to core - was really unsure after turning Great Yarmouth about how I was going to get back, but dogged determination and help from Lakenheath who allowed me to climb away in their ATZ and Cambridge who routed traffic around me (the transponder helps) as I climbed in their extended centre line mean I got back despite some gloom and the wind, which thankfully did die a bit.
From heavy 18m yesterday to light 15m today - challenging conditions, but satisfying.

afternoon bimble to the nice Cu in Norfolk - 
Marham helpful with a MATZ clearance, then they got busy and I had to make a quick exit
Fabulous run into Whittington and then down to Didcot - frustrating on the way home, struggled to find the good cores.  Often the strong cores were very narrow, and even in 15m hard to stay in.

I wasn’t the fastest, but I had fun 😀
Day off tomorrow !

Mixed!
Great climb off tow and before the start meant we had to dive and go through the start at speed, but after that only found scrappy things into Soham and out making us think we had started too early. After we got back in the running, generally better - fickle climbs like in previous days, but the odd much better one. After a scrabble near Eastwell, then a brilliant run down a street on the leg to Calvert. Blue'ing out around Calvert and into MK - tried a few things which didn't work, but the edge of MK did (as Richard had expected) - then a straightforward run home with the best climb of the day at Bedford, meaning we took it over glide and raced in.

All in all, a nice day out!  Thank you to Richard for the company.
Didn’t set off until too late so cut the task short.
Cloud Rally Racer Task 3

Fantastic day out! 
Task suggested by Robert Theil. Great task for the day.

Difficult to find thermals. When you found a thermal it was mostly broken and weak. I was quite happy to have made it to Northampton West. Found one good climb on the way back, then nothing until Gransden. I was looking to abandon but found a climb so I continued the grind. Still some Cu towards the East but this would've been very hard work to fly Elmswell W and especially back to Gransden with the strong wind. I didn't find any climb so turned around/abandoned when still at final glide back to Gransden. Not my kind of weather but happy with the distance I flew. 
Big congrats to 899 who completed his task!

Good first leg. Only weaker climbs were found on way home where I expected higher climb rates. Should've deviated further with the patchy looking sky to the good looking clouds North and South of track. Still was lucky to find climbs staying on track and climbed up to 800ft above final glide. Only negative netto air was found on final to the point that I didn't think it was safe to continue final glide. So the engine was started while being 300ft above final glide close to the airport.

Was a bit worried about the bits of areas of zero XC speed from 13:00 and later to the north of GRL according to Skysight. So I set this task avoiding just that. Mostly pretty good weather, not always easy to find the right climbs, some spread out clouds. Took a cloud climb, wasn't as useful as I picked up some thin/light ice on the leading edge. Sky still looked soarable hours later.
Occasionally tricky with some strong climbs if you could find them.

Based task on Skysight forecast and highest speed for task. It all was a bit more difficult than I expected. Relative low cloud base. Dire looking sky from Leicester to Rugeley. But I always found a climb when I needed one. Sky opened up a bit towards the West/Wales, but never as good as originally was forecast. Near Brecon the terrain came up but the cloud base didn't. Was looking soft near Pontypool, had to make some zig zags to avoid lee/sink. After Pontypool it was difficult to find a climb, many scrappy soft clouds didn't work. Luckily I found a climb in the blue and from there on I managed to get to a good working height to get to the better looking clouds from Malvern onward. Thermals were getting further between them but managed to get ok on final glide. East Anglia still looking bubbly after landing.
Flight after working in the morning. Fairly straight forward to Bungay. On the way back spread out clouds and difficult to find climbs.

First cross country flight on this new type glider for me. Got stuck behind Cranfield ATZ, took some time before ATC allowed me to cross and enter their ATZ. 
Skysight forecast was too pessimistic for today. TM and RASP more accurate today.

I knew today's weather made the task difficult. I attempted to do a BUG-MEN task for a trophy route for CGC; otherwise, I would have chosen a different task.

It was a fairly good run to BUG, but on the way back east, there were fewer route options. The best weather was always in the airspace around Birmingham and Sywell, but the airspace above was limiting. I was happy to reach East Anglia. The only issue was that the clouds and thermals didn't extend far enough south to MEN. I reached MEN but didn't have enough altitude to fly west back to the lift (15 km or more). My only option was to continue east to some scrappy-looking clouds. Just before I planned to start the engine, I found some lift. From there, it was a struggle to go 100 km into up to 20 knots of wind. I'm very happy to have made it back again.

I just completed my first 750 km! It was more challenging than expected.

The first leg was tough with some top cover Finding a path towards Shepton Mallet was hard due to large blue gaps in the sky. On the way back to Caxton Gibbet, I hit a low point near Milton Keynes. Luckily, I found a climb after making a short 180-degree turn.

The next leg southwest was straightforward until Swindon. After that, it became a struggle with no significant climbs. The leg back to Gransden looked promising, but the best clouds were in Brize airspace. I thought I’d need to start my engine just past Abingdon, but I found a climb over the town that took me to over 5200 ft. Then I had to glide 50 km through dead air to some of the last active clouds beyond Milton Keynes. Just before reaching Milton Keynes, I found a climb over Woburn Golf Club at 19:15 local time, which took me to the final glide.

There was some lift on the way home, but the sky was almost dead. I'm very happy to have finished this task!

Wasn't planning on flying until the forecast changed this morning so was a bit later at the airfield. Great conditions all around. Was forecast to be a bit weaker around Calvert but it was actually better. Final glide of 85km!
Cloud Rally Racer Task 1 Windicapped task. Better than expected conditions.

Pretty good energy lines to Whittington and to Abingdon, then it was bit blue/soft to Wantage. I struggled to find the good climbs on the way back. Had to make a big deviation to take a climb to final glide. Very enjoyable flight.
First XC of the season, a modest attempt at the club's 100 km task. Abandoned after turning Rushden in overdeveloped conditions. Good timing let down by poor task-setting.
I finally completed a second 300 km task, this time with just two turnpoints, hopefully qualifying for Diamond Goal, certification pending. Thanks to Wenshu for the clever task design.
Finally! Gold Height, after 15 years of gliding, mostly in East Anglia. Perhaps that was my error?
Another task cut short by the motor. At least until then I was making faster progress than is usual for me.
Unreadable clouds and poor visibility proved too much for me. I was rarely above 3,000 feet, and then only just. I would surely have landed out at Lyveden if not for the motor.
Third XC task of the season, yet again requiring the motor. My completion rate was better when the consequences were harsher!

I should have followed the flock going east/west.  Lots of spreadout and overcast in Grantham area.  On the way back in survival mode I abandoned thoughts of the last (Olney) TP just hoping to get back to GRL.  At Connington, now in superb conditions, I decided not to be a wimp and reinstated it.  Not going direct from DRL added about 50km to the task!
Better than I expected.  Abandoned declared task at Northampton West and kept going to Edge Hill.  Cloud streets mainly good but top cover made things weak around Banbury.
Orginal plan to Norfolk looked poor due top cover so set off undeclared to follow the weather and the pack.  Nice 65 km glide from near Eastwell to Whittlesea.
Was originally a 200 km task but abandoned going to two TPs because it became very soft.  At least we were flying, it can only get better.
Sky died north of Pitsford, so discretion rather than valour.
Most enjoyable.
CGC cloud rally day 5

Didn't carry out original task, launched a bit too late. Found it more difficult than I expected, good climbs but hard to find. The brilliant visibility and the cockpit ventilation linkage disconnecting on max air added to the fun!
Silver distance flight, slow, but a blue day.

Good on the first leg with lovely views over the Wash and north Norfolk coast. Thermals got progressively more tricky on the second leg, with one thermal containing the eclectic mix of me, a paraglider, a buzzard and a gull all going round together. On the third leg there was an area of spread out which I didn't emerge from until after I had got onto final glide!

Very good at the start of the first leg, blue hole before the TP, difficult to get back from Silverstone.
Enjoyed the 15m roll rate!

Enjoyed flying to Glastonbury although it wss really 20km too far due to what I took to be sea air causing it to blue out. But then a lapse in concentration led to an engine start near Milton Keynes so it back home for tea.

SkySight said this 500km was on though the southerly wind direction suggested managing expectations. 

Tricky in East Anglia. The airmass really wasn't into the convection thing and was pretty grumpy about being made to do it. Result was gusty, disorganised thermals. Got low on multiple occasions and even had the wheel down. By the time I got back to the club it was apparent that I was unlikely to be making Burley Gate, but I thought I'd see how far I could reasonably get while allowing time to get back. At Northampton the air improved somewhat and the climbs became more reliable, but even so I managed to miss some and get low on the way back. Under the circs I was pleased to get 400km out of it.

First cross-country for pre-solo Adam who flew very well, coring a number of thermals on his own. We decided to have a crack at least of the start of the day's task. We took it very cautiously upwind as we really didn't want to land out and we spent some time admiring Ely Cathedral which was looking fabulous in the sun. Coming back the sky still looked so good ahead that I couldn't resist seeing if we could make it the rest of the way to the club without turning and we did indeed manage to change a height deficit into a generous surplus by bouncing the climbs over most of the return distance. Nevertheless we couldn't quite keep up with 871 who we 'caught up' at the turnpoint. :-)

Slightly slow start, but then conditions became really good. Great views of new country for me. I found the convergence area at the far end of the task tricky and took a few weak climbs and diversions to stay in the game. It was fantastic to reconnect with the good stuff in a 6-7 knot climb over Longleat.
Just when it was starting to get murky again after Oxford I hit another unexpected convergence with some curtain cloud which was very helpful to get on to final glide.
A most enjoyable flight - perhaps I should keep the Ventus? :-)

Late start but then was able to follow forming cumulus on the first leg. Eastern end of the task on the high cumulus but slow return on the last leg in the blue.

The Ventus is still for sale!

Gusty thermals on the first leg. The second leg was under top cover until Telford when the sky came alive again. After the Mynd it was back under the top cover so I dropped the water only for it to turn ballistic all the way home!
Thanks to SJ for mentioning the task. I had thought about it but wondered if it was too much for the day. Thanks to him also for marking a very helpful climb after the Mynd.
First cross-country for Ed who did a decent chunk of the flying including a long final glide in energy lines on the last leg.
Underset as it turned out. Gusty thermals but streeting well in places.

Another day, another bridge.  Humber yesterday and Severn today.  Lovely conditions except for the last 20km or so in and out of Thornbury which slowed me down a lot. Luckily got a good climb just before Thornbury and was able to tip-toe in and out and back to the better weather. Stonking climb just when I needed it approaching Thrapston, which got me the rest of the way home.
It got very iffy west of Swindon, but I decided to press on to Chippenham. Had to use the jet to get back to the good air again.
Slow start then things got progressively better.  Was on final glide, but took a final top up just to be safe. Lovely days flying..
Previous submission had wrong glider type. The flight was in the Duo

A long slow struggle upwind, with low cloud-base until I got to North Lincolnshire.  Things then improved (though I got rained on at the Scunthorpe turnpoint) and all went well until things died near Peterborough and I decided to get the engine out early.
Very slow, but something I have wanted to do for some time.
A streamlined version of the Cloud Rally's Racer task to inspect the track and see if Skysight was right. It was.
A struggle most of the time, though some good energy lines occasionally. thanks to Cambridge Radar who allowed me to route over the top on the way home.

I achieved the main objective, to see the sea.  The last 20km in and out of Cromer was tricky, but once I got back to a sensible looking sky I felt I could relax. But no, the clouds looked great but I had great trouble finding the lift. I eventually had to start up the jet and create my own thermal...
Needles to say, once I got going again, the rest of the trip home was easy.
ICL Pundits.  Set as DHT but scored here as normal task
Should have abandoned Gainsborough, which was under thick cloud, but hoped to find a climb over the town or the power station. Didn't.

Started late in the day with a fast downwind dash to the Humber estuary. Searched for a good climb before crossing the river to Thorngumbald, and was rewarded with 5knots to around 7,000ft.  But conditions deteriorated on the return leg and I had to sawtooth with the turbo to get across the dead air. Things picked up around Spalding and I was able to get home with 2/3 of fuel unused.
Set a shortish task as I thought it would be harder than it was.  Lovely save from about 1000 feet just south of Peterborough - I've no idea where it came from, but I was very pleased to find it!
Well, that was a character building exercise!  But at least we were flying.
Late start after work in the morning. Launched 2.20pm. Pleased with the distance. Sea air hindered last leg a bit. Plus faffing
First proper flight this season. Very satisfying and everyone had a good time and got round.

A great flight but chose the easy way back when it got tough. 
Nice view of the 100 ft drain - flooded

Cambridge  Cloud rally task so manually adjusted to get it to score as shape of sectors not standard.
Task was well set and enjoyable. Thankyou to Lakenheath for clearance of large number of gliders into the Matz between Bury and Methwold.
Fettling followed by a cross country without water ballast just to check it all works. Nice conditions and the day was longer and better than most people thought
Started really late because I had to work in the morning...quite enjoyable in places in others not so much :)
Wasn't really planning to go cross-country as it was the first flight with the glider after the winter but I just couldn't help it. Thanks David for the task.
A lovely days flying
Phil Atkin did the fast bits :-)
Lovely flight for what must be getting near the end of the season

Bit slow to start with, rain north or Newark and rain at Peterborough on the way back.  Abandoned going to Hus Bos on the way back. Some good climbs in the middle. Landed at Upwood rather than stick it in a wet field. Thanks to Andy Roch for the aerotow back. Oddly nobody else flew cross country from Gransden :-)

Lovely flight with scenic leg down the East Anglian Coast.  Some clouds were working over the sea near Lowestoft. Further south there were sections of sea breeze front. Good to put the band back together.
Got to love a 27 with water.
Junior Nationals Day 1
Junior Nationals Day 2
Junior Day 4
Wasn't planning on flying but rigged, declared and launched. Almost landed out at Saltby due to top cover but made it round and finished my first 300.
Junior Nationals Day 5

Should have gone further east!  Started a bit late, and struggled slowly for a couple of hours under complete top cover.  Eventually the conditions improved, but it was time to turn for home.  Fortunately, the large shower lines I passed on both legs were a little to the west.
Kept it to 300 since I was doing evening flying.
Really strong day - and very enjoyable.  Threatened to blue out a bit on the last leg but was OK.  The task managed to miss the big patches of cirrus floating around.
Good to have a good day and complete a task for once!

A great street almost to Bedford, but a struggle to get out of Bedford which required a slow climb.  Then improving conditions into East Anglia.  On the way back, at Bury the air got very murky again, and climbs became harder to find.  A bit of a scrabble near Cambridge after which I was above glide, and in an energy line.

Bedford, the first turnpoint was very difficult - broken thermals which were hard to find.  Conditions improved as I went east, and the run from Great Yarmouth to about Newmarket was delightful with really strong streets.  After Newmarket overcast, and eventually the climbs became weak and with the strong wind I couldn't make any progress - in and out of the Cambridge ATZ for 50 mins (thanks Cambridge - who were really helpful).  So had to start the engine for the last 20km.  Good fun though.
Crowland was difficult and I fell out - that's why we have an engine boys and girls!
Nice flight with 700, 899, 871 and M8
Fun day out.  Interesting mixture of conditions, with the run into Eastwell very congested, but strong elsewhere
Low and slow - but worth doing.  Probably a bit cautious, but aiming not to get too low.

Some quite big diversions off track to stay out of the blue - but some good runs even so.

Blued out east of Bedford, but enough haze caps to be reasonably easy.
Really nice day, especially after yesterday.  East Anglia stayed strong well into the afternoon.
Decided to see the seaside.  First couple of legs good, but Blakeney - Framlingham a grovel.  Norwich gave a transit without issue.
Somwhat late after lunch start, and a bit blue to start with.  But picked up around Bury, and fairly straightforward after that.
Strong day
We ran into a large congested area and discretion seemed the order of the day, so abandoned.

Went early because I thought the day might be short.  A bit soft on the way out.  Got stuck at Great Malvern in survival mode for 20 mins.  Reasonable trip back, but last 30 km a bit tricky since I was dead on glide and needed to stop at St Neots for a very weak climb.  But worth doing.
Really nice day to go flying.  A little broken low, but climbs to 5500 ft.  Howden Dam very pretty.
Thanks to Chris Lewis for the idea - inspired given the weather to the West.

Decided to play with the forecast convergence on the east coast.

Went early to avoid the turnpoints being in the sea air, but had to grovel on the way out.  Good run up and down the coast, but routing poorly through the spreadout on the way home and had to go into survival mode.
First flight of 2024.
A fun flight. Conditions better than I expected and the day went on for a couple of hours after landing.
Good areas and soft areas. Turned back about 12k from BSE. Rather stupid of me as the day had plenty left in it. Still, a fun flight.

Started early as forecast suggested an early cut-off. Weak climbs early on then OK until about 40k from GRM when cloud-base came down by 1000 feet and it became soft. The run home was OK but the sky ahead didn't look good about 60kms out from home on the return leg so flew rather too cautiously. A better day than expected.

Very weak most of the way. The clag from the NM was over Corby. Tried running under the clag but it was completely dead so turned short of the turn whilst I had enough height to get back to the sun. A fun day. thanks Paul (28) for the task.
A much better day than expected. Screwed up the leg into Broadway. Should have routed further south into the sunny bits. Otherwise a super flight.
A difficult day. A few decent climbs but had to take many weak ones. Clouds not reliable. Still, it was good to fly after 2 weeks of poor weather.
A struggle in the strong wind to EDG. Mainly broken up climbs with the odd good core. Better conditions in the east.

A challenging day. High cover from about Oxford. Very soft near Devizes. Some good climbs on second leg. Hard work on last leg. Although tough it was a really fun flight. Not the best task for the day.
First and second legs good, but high cover for the rest of the flight dampened the thermals. Still, another super day.
Very slow for much of the flight. Not all clouds delivered and those that did were often quite weak. Had my best climb at ELY with about 50k to go. Even so, a fun flight.
First leg slow as conditions quite weak. Second leg OK. Very soft at Manton and pretty much survival mode all the way home apart from a good climb at Grafham Water
A decent run to BUG, The return leg was rather more difficult due to spread-out/high cover so had to divert quite a lot. Shared several thermals with 700.

A reasonable run to BIC, and onto Melton Mowbray. There was little sun on the ground but decent cores could be found. With 12kms to go to NWK I could have arrived at the TP at 3000 feet where 205 was marking a weak climb but didn't have the desire to continue. So had a gentle trip home.
Super conditions until within 20k of Sheffield when I hit sink of 12kts. Quite turbulent and broken up thermals near Sheffield. Second half of the return trip strong cores once again.

what made today was the really good polar airmass on the spring easterly
any hint of sun and it was working
looked awful at GRL (8/8) so took off and started the task to see how far I could get before starting the engine - turns out all the way round  !!
low and overcast to MK then perked up a bit to BIC and northampton where it got sticky and had my grubby mitts hovering over the engine switches once or twice then nice after that and a romp on the way home

blue conditions east of the M1 on the way out and returning home so lost some time there otherwise a bit of a blast in the middle of the country (apart from a fumble after turning BUG before I crept back over the Malverns) - more please

late start then bang - high base cumulus in the task area - had to fly blueish to get to NOS but then Ok - nasty glide from Eyebrook to Skeffington and back with zero convection and cocked up the rest of the home leg by believing the big clouds would deliver ...  they didn't !!
GRL cloud rally - not a cloud in the sky !!

GRL cloud rally
Lorna hadn't flown in a while so nice to get some km behind her - we were tasked to fly over her work-place and house - three times - LOL
GRL cloud rally

trouble with a 300k FAI triangle in east anglia is that you have to deal with sea air... twice ! and as for a track line through chatteris and Anmer Hall with a 3000' base - I was rude to William on the way through - don't think he heard me !
slow tricky day, engine switches set for a start in the A11 murk after Diss - didn't need it, sensible last leg

trying to get out and back before the sky disintegrated - failed !
great run to TIB - brisk tailwind great streets and honest clouds
really tricky run home - had only 2 decent climbs on the way back
a voice in the back said my oudie reckons if we were an ASW 29 we'd get home from here !
ran into the murk at Cambridge and (DT pf) failed to tip toe through a so nearly soarable sky -shucks
so chatted to Cambridge and fired up in their ATZ
Left early due to the spreadout forecast.  In hindsight leaving an hr or 2 later would have improved the speed.
Well, the forecasts didn't agree and we ended up flying in the best conditions I have experienced for a long time. The glider seems to go really well in 15m.
North of Peterborough it was difficult but after an epic climb from low it got better and better.
At last a stress free flight with the GRL gang, excellent conditions.
Top cover for most of the day but the perfect task for the day.
The day of the year for Gransden pilots!
Another character building flight.  Only 2 of us mad enough to fly xc from GRL.
A few good bits, a few blue bits, a few soft bits.  Come on 2024, give us some proper soaring weather!
this was painful
Rat race but a little slow on the way back due to pilot error.
I didn’t anticipate the spread out after turning Olney so abandoned.
Left a bit too early worried about the day not lasting as long as it did.  In the end we could have done another 100K.  A glorious gliding day!
Great Malvern again but good fun with the usual suspects.
A fun day out with the usual Gransden crowd.
A great day in the Peak District with 700 and 28.
Decided on a 400K FAI triangle rather then a bigger task up and down the streets.  A bit soft early on but the day improved nicely once past Peterborough.
Late out due to fathers day breakfast.  Excellent conditions, more please.
Nice to be back in the air.
A few good climbs where there was sun on the ground but mainly another grind.
A nice flying day but visibility not so nice.  Excellent task setting by 28.
At last a good day for GRL.
A good day out with the GRL crowd.  In and out of Eastwell was overcast which slowed things down considerably.
Cirrus slowed things down until Easwell but after that it was excellent.

Although it seemed very unlikely that R313 (the Scampton Red Arrows restricted area) would be active, it was difficult to get confirmation. Waddington ATC wasn't answering either of the 'phone numbers given in the chart note at 9:30 AM on a Saturday; nor were Waddington, Coningsby or Cranwell approach answering on the radio. In the end Humberside LARS provided confirmation while I was still 40 Km to their south, and also an admirable Basic Service with plenty of traffic information in the Humber Bridge area (which was surprisingly busy). I didn't have the Northern Frequency Reference Card" with me (rookie mistake)
File created on: but of course the Humberside LARS channel (119.130) is also on the chart. And I did indeed dip into R313 for a few turns of a thermal on the return leg.

Club Name: there was no lift to speak of south of Peterborough on the way home
Surname: so took the highest climb I could under the last cloud
Forename: then did the last 45 Km at best glide for 25 minutes
FlightID: husbanding every last foot of arrival height.

Club Name: Humberside Approach and whoever wrote the LK8000 arrival height code."

Notes: A good day by any standards, and amazingly good for mid-September.
A late start, and only a couple of hours available before an evening appointment, so ambled round 160 km in the blue.

15:20 launch at Borders GC. Weak wave to start with, but later connected with higher system that gave improved climb rates. Needed to land because of approaching sunset. Hopefully more wave tomorrow :-)
Another wave flight from Borders GC with great views. Wave strength 1 to 2kts with occasional 3kts.
Another wave flight at Milfield including a scenic tour of Northumberland (and a bit of Scotland). Thanks to all at Borders GC for your hospitality.

Flight to check out re-installed LX after repair. Joined Gransden gang on GRM O/R. Good climbs around, but soft around BAN on the way home. Deviated further than expected along street to get good climb onto final glide.
Late start (14:40) for 300km. Ballistic 2/3 up 1st leg so thought it was on. Going south it progressively got weaker. Aborted at Northampton - sea air probably from just after BOZ to GRL.
Declared, but not on logger as using cobbled together instrumentation as LX flight computer/vario at LXNAV for repair. Clouds  very variable. Best climbs encountered in the blue to the west.
Much better conditions than expected. 5250ft cloudbase in East Anglia and streeting. Needed to deviate off track to find climbs at times.
Enjoyable flight with club-mates. Conditions generally ok, but I made a complete horlicks crossing Milton Keynes both times to/from DEV and wasted time. Top cover affected final leg BAN -> GRL.

Just became soarable when I launched. Nothing planned so just an extended local bimble. Went to St Neots first, but not in sector. Good climbs around -  high cover and thus weak climbs made it interesting coming back from LYV. Fun flight.
Good fun was had but I probably set the task in the wrong area. Clouds looked so much better elsewhere, just not in my direction of travel.
Good energy line and strong climbs on the way out to the north, but the clouds were rapidly disappearing and getting more unreliable. Lucky to be back home!
Little bimble for the first day of the soaring season

Today was such hard work and it turned into a game of survival after turning Corby. Horrible vis, couldn't see what was coming up...I was scraping around near Lyveden for good amount of time before finally climbing away and then decided that was enough for the day. Thanks to Lyveden base for the helpful information while I was struggling!

Snail speed on the first leg to Lyveden - for some reason I just really failed to connect with the thermals properly despite the good looking clouds. The moment I turned Lyveden things suddenly improved (or maybe the pilot), strong climbs and easy all the way to BSE then back home!

1st and 2nd leg went as planned, but the upwind leg going to Calvert Junction was such a pain - slow, lots of mistakes, couldn't centre the thermals, and nearly gave up a million times. Turweston was helpful when I asked if I could land there, and then I climbed away. Coming home was straightforward even at 6 pm, and I just wish the entire flight was like that. Finally a 300k flight hopefully qualifying for diamond goal, and I made sure to cross the 1km start/finish line unlike the last 300k I did, which a painful lesson to learn...
